title: Collinsonia elsholtzioides
intro: Collinsonia elsholtzioides, commonly known as Rich's Collinsonia or Stone Root, is a perennial plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The health benefits of Collinsonia elsholtzioides include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of varicose veins, hemorrhoids, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Collinsonia elsholtzioides include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Collinsonia elsholtzioides is classified within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other plants in this family, such as its floral structure and leaf morphology. The genus Collinsonia is further grouped within the tribe Clinopodiumeae, which includes other plants with similar characteristics. Collinsonia elsholtzioides is a perennial herb native to North America, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this region. The classification of Collinsonia elsholtzioides is based on a combination of morphological and molecular evidence.
